source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/260213 태국 MD Healthcard Co., Ltd/260327 추가보완서류/1.2 [Portable X-ray system] Technical Document/[Attachment] 4. Summary of Design Verification and Validation/4.1 Laboratory ISO17025 Cetificate/3. [KTR] ISOIEC 17025 Certificate.pdf

document.title: ISO/IEC 17025 Certificate Scope No. KT011
document.documentType: Laboratory ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation certificate scope / testing scope table

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk lists the accredited electrical testing scope under certificate No. KT011, covering battery, metering, industrial machinery, household appliance, EV charging, audio/video/ICT safety, and related regulatory standards with test ranges and field-testing status.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Can support regulatory submissions by documenting that the laboratory scope includes specific electrical safety, performance, EMC-exception, battery, machinery, household appliance, EV charging, and metering standards and their measurable ranges.
keyPoints: Battery standards include portable and industrial lithium cell/battery safety and performance standards such as KS C IEC 62133-2, 62619, 62620, IEC 62660-1, KS R 1204, and battery energy storage SPS standards.

Measuring instrument scope includes DIN/EN/IEC/OIML electricity metering standards and Korean MOTIE type approval notices, generally up to AC 600 V, DC 1500 V, AC 160 A, and DC 500 A depending on item.

Industrial machinery scope includes EN/KS IEC 60204-1, NFPA 79, SEMI S2 and SEMI S22 electrical safety tests such as bonding continuity, insulation resistance, voltage testing, residual voltage, leakage current, dielectric, temperature, and sound pressure tests.

Household appliance scope includes IEC/KC 60335 series and related IEC 60065, 60950-1, 61851, and 62368-1 standards with voltage, current, temperature, withstand voltage, humidity, glow wire, tracking, and heat resistance ranges.

entities.name: No. KT011
entities.evidence: Repeated page headers identify the scope as "No. KT011" across pages 501/1187 through 669/1187.

entities.name: MOLIT
entities.evidence: MOLIT Notice No. 2025-73 is listed for Detailed Regulations of Motor Vehicle Safety Standards, Attachment 1 No. 48 safety test for drive battery.

entities.name: MOTIE
entities.evidence: MOTIE notices are listed for Regulatory Safety Confirmation Standards and type approval standards for watt-hour meters and electric vehicle charging systems.

entities.name: OIML
entities.evidence: OIML R 46-1/-2:2012 is listed for active electrical energy meters metrological and technical requirements and performance tests.

entities.name: SEMI
entities.evidence: SEMI S2-0724 and SEMI S22-0718E are listed under electrical machinery for industries.

entities.name: NFPA
entities.evidence: NFPA 79:2024 is listed as Electrical Standard for Industrial Machinery.

concepts.name: Lithium battery safety testing scope
concepts.evidence: KS C IEC 62133-2:2021, KS C IEC 62619:2017/2022, and KS C IEC 62620:2014/2023 are listed with DC voltage/current, power, temperature, and force ranges.

concepts.name: Drive battery regulatory safety testing
concepts.evidence: MOLIT Notice No. 2025-73 lists Attachment 1 No. 48 safety tests for drive battery, including external short circuit, overcharge, over-discharge, over-temperature, and over-current protection.

concepts.name: Electricity metering test accreditation
concepts.evidence: DIN EN, EN, IEC, KS, OIML, and MOTIE standards for electricity metering equipment and watt-hour meters are listed with AC/DC voltage and current ranges.

concepts.name: Machinery electrical safety verification
concepts.evidence: EN 60204-1:2018, KS C IEC 60204-1:2016, NFPA 79:2024, SEMI S2-0724, and SEMI S22-0718E list continuity, insulation resistance, voltage, residual voltage, leakage current, dielectric, and temperature tests.

concepts.name: Household appliance safety testing scope
concepts.evidence: IEC and KC 60335 series standards are listed for household and similar appliances with voltage, current, temperature, withstand voltage, humidity, heat resistance, glow wire, and tracking ranges.

concepts.name: EV charging system testing scope
concepts.evidence: IEC 61851-1:2010, IEC 61851-1:2017, and IEC 61851-23:2014 are listed for electric vehicle conductive charging systems and DC charging stations.

evidence.claim: The accreditation scope includes portable lithium cell and battery testing under KS C IEC 62133-2:2021.
evidence.evidenceText: KS C IEC 62133-2:2021 lists safety requirements for portable sealed secondary lithium cells and batteries, lithium systems, with DC voltage 0-100 V, current 0-400 A, temperature -40 to 150 C, and force 0-30 kN.

evidence.claim: The scope includes industrial lithium battery safety testing under KS C IEC 62619:2017 and 62619:2022.
evidence.evidenceText: KS C IEC 62619:2017 and KS C IEC 62619:2022 are listed for secondary lithium cells and batteries for industrial applications with DC voltage 0-1500 V, current 0-660 A, power 0-320 kW, temperature -40 to 150 C, and force 0-30 kN.

evidence.claim: The scope includes industrial lithium battery performance testing under KS C IEC 62620:2014 and 62620:2023.
evidence.evidenceText: KS C IEC 62620:2014 and 62620:2023 are listed for secondary lithium cells and batteries for industrial applications with DC voltage 0-1500 V, current 0-660 A, power 0-320 kW, and temperature -40 to 150 C.

evidence.claim: The scope includes electric-road-vehicle lithium-ion cell performance testing under KS C IEC62660-1:2018.
evidence.evidenceText: KS C IEC62660-1:2018 lists secondary lithium-ion cells for propulsion of electric road vehicles, Part 1 performance testing, with DC voltage 0-5 V, current 0-400 A, and temperature -20 to 80 C.

evidence.claim: The scope includes electric bus traction battery pack and system performance testing under KS R 1204:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: KS R 1204:2019 lists secondary lithium-ion traction battery packs and systems for electric buses, performance requirements and test methods, with DC voltage 0-1500 V, current 0-660 A, power 0-320 kW, and temperature -40 to 150 C.

evidence.claim: The scope includes drive battery protection tests under MOLIT Notice No. 2025-73.
evidence.evidenceText: MOLIT Notice No. 2025-73 lists Detailed Regulations of Motor Vehicle Safety Standards, Attachment 1 No. 48, including external short circuit, overcharge, over discharge, over-temperature, and over-current protection tests.

evidence.claim: The measuring-instrument scope includes electricity metering equipment tests under EN/IEC 62052 and 62053 series standards.
The metering scope includes stated EMC-related exceptions for several electricity metering standards.
evidence.evidenceText: EN IEC 62052-11 and IEC 62052-11 entries list exceptions including voltage dips, electrostatic discharge, radiated RF electromagnetic field immunity, fast transient/burst, conducted disturbances, surge, ring wave, damped oscillatory wave, and emission requirements.

evidence.claim: The scope includes Korean type approval standards for watt-hour meters and electric vehicle charging systems under MOTIE notices.
evidence.evidenceText: MOTIE Notice No. 2020-230 and No. 2025-149 are listed for watt-hour meter type approval; MOTIE Notice No. 2022-164 and No. 2024-115 are listed for electric vehicle charging system type approval.

evidence.claim: The industrial machinery scope includes EN 60204-1:2018 electrical equipment of machines tests.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60204-1:2018 lists verification of protective bonding continuity, insulation resistance tests, voltage tests, and protection against residual voltages, with max 60 A bonding continuity, DC 500 V insulation resistance, AC 1500 V/DC 2121 V voltage test, and residual voltage max 600 V.

evidence.claim: The industrial machinery scope includes KS C IEC 60204-1:2016 with the same electrical safety verification categories.
evidence.evidenceText: KS C IEC 60204-1:2016 lists continuity of protective bonding circuit, insulation resistance, voltage tests, and protection against residual voltages with the same stated ranges.

evidence.claim: The scope includes power electronic converter safety testing under IEC 62477-1:2012 and KS C IEC 62477-1:2016.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62477-1:2012 and KS C IEC 62477-1:2016 list safety requirements for power electronic converter systems and equipment with DC voltage max 1500 V, DC current max +/-330 A, AC voltage max 305 V, AC current max 360 A, temperature -20 to 60 C, and humidity max 95 percent.

evidence.claim: The scope includes NFPA 79:2024 industrial machinery electrical testing.
evidence.evidenceText: NFPA 79:2024 lists continuity of effective ground-fault current path, insulation resistance tests, voltage test, and protection against residual voltages with bonding continuity max 60 A and voltage test AC 1500 V/DC 2121 V.

evidence.claim: The scope includes SEMI safety guideline testing for semiconductor manufacturing equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: SEMI S2-0724 lists earthing continuity and protective bonding circuit testing and sound pressure level; SEMI S22-0718E lists leakage current, earthing continuity, input, dielectric, temperature, and finger probe tests.

evidence.claim: The household appliance scope includes IEC 60335-1 general safety testing for household and similar electrical appliances.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60335-1 ed5.0:2010 and ed6.0:2020 list voltage single phase max 300 V, three phase max 600 V, current max 20 A, temperature max 300 C, withstand voltage max 4 kV, humidity max 95 +/-3 percent RH, heat resistance max 150 C, glow wire max 960 C, and tracking max 600 V.

evidence.claim: The household appliance scope includes audio/video and ICT safety standards IEC 60065, IEC 60950-1, and IEC 62368-1.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60065, IEC 60950-1, and IEC 62368-1 ed3.0:2018 are listed for audio/video, information technology, and communication technology equipment safety requirements with voltage, current, temperature, and humidity ranges.

evidence.claim: The scope includes electric vehicle conductive charging system testing under IEC 61851-1 and IEC 61851-23.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 61851-1:2010, IEC 61851-1:2017, and IEC 61851-23:2014 are listed for EV conductive charging systems and DC EV charging stations with single/three phase voltage max 450 V, DC voltage max 1000 V, AC current max 800 A, DC current max 500 A, temperature -50 to 350 C, and humidity max 96 percent.

evidence.claim: Multiple KC 60335 household appliance standards in 2025 revisions are included in the scope.
KC 60335-2-29:2025 battery charger testing appears with two revision dates.
evidence.evidenceText: KC 60335-2-29:2025 is listed for battery chargers with Revision 05.22.2025 and a separate entry with Revision 10.27.2025.

evidence.claim: Some household appliance standards list explicit exceptions from the accredited scope.
evidence.evidenceText: KC 60335-2-82:2022 lists exceptions for 22.106 and Annex R Software evaluation; KC 60335-2-84:2022 lists an excluded resistance to rusting item 31.101; IEC/KC 60335-2-79 lists exceptions for heating, abnormal operation, stability and mechanical hazards.

reviewFlags.reason: Chunk begins mid-table and ends mid-entry, so document-level title/date and complete scope are inferred only from the visible certificate identifier, source path, and listed table content.
The source is a partial OCR/text extraction with spacing and line-break artifacts; standard designations were normalized only where directly readable from the chunk.
